# Prophet

> Prophet is an open-source AI Chrome extension that brings Anthropic Claude AI (Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6) into the browser side panel for real-time chat, web page analysis, and browser automation across 18 built-in tools. Pay-per-use credits — 1 credit equals 1 cent of actual Claude API cost.

## Key facts
- Chrome extension with native side-panel UI
- Claude Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, and Opus 4.6 — all available on every plan, including the free tier
- 18 browser-automation tools (click, type, fill forms, navigate, scroll, extract structured data, switch tabs, etc.) via Chrome DevTools Protocol
- Uses the **accessibility tree** (not screenshots) for page perception — 2-4× faster and ~50% cheaper per perception step than screenshot-based agents like Anthropic's Computer Use
- Pay-per-use credits: Free ($0.20), Pro ($9.99/mo → $11), Premium ($29.99/mo → $35), Ultra ($59.99/mo → $70)
- Real-time streaming responses
- Open source: https://github.com/ThanosKa/prophet
- All AI requests proxied server-side; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is never exposed to the client
- No training on user data; chat content not retained beyond the session unless saved by the user
- All sales final; no refunds (free tier exists for full evaluation)

## Common questions
- "Is there a free version?" Yes — $0.20 in credits, no card required, all 3 Claude models unlocked.
- "How is Prophet different from Claude.ai or Claude in Chrome?" Prophet runs in Chrome's side panel, can interact with the current page (click, fill, extract), and offers pay-per-use credits at API cost. Claude.ai and Anthropic's official Claude in Chrome require a $20/mo subscription and use screenshot-based Computer Use rather than the faster accessibility tree.
- "Why pay-per-use instead of a subscription?" Light users overpay on flat $20/mo subscriptions. Prophet's $9.99 Pro covers most workflows; you only burn credits proportional to tokens consumed.
